results.rationale: The salt context, sodium-ion test prompt and two labelled response fields are visible; the flame-test/yellow-flame key and model answer fit both marks.
The chloride-ion prompt and labelled test/result fields are clear, and the acidified silver nitrate plus white precipitate evidence grades the visible task.
The full evaporation-to-constant-mass method is visible, and the answer key correctly links constant mass to complete water removal.
The prior method and all four choices are visible; subtracting the empty-dish mass from the dry dish-and-contents mass is present and correctly keyed.
Table 1 supplies all four sodium chloride concentrations and its g/dm³ heading, the 39.3% sodium value is visible, and the response carries the final unit; the 13.8 g/dm³ model is correct.
The inspected Figure 1 clearly shows Pipe P returning material from the condenser to the reactor, and the recycling key matches that visible process.
The Haber-process reaction is identified in the visible context, the free-response line is usable, and iron is the correct catalyst key.
The oxygen-test task is self-contained with separate test and result fields; glowing splint and relighting are correctly paired in the grading evidence.
The word equation supplies every required substance name and inspected Figure 2 is a complete annotatable reaction-profile canvas; the activation-energy and side-label criteria fit it.
The catalysed profile is visible and all four options render; the higher-peak option is present and correctly keyed for removal of the catalyst.
The ammonium nitrate setup and open response are clear, and the accepted uses, including fertiliser, are supported by the model and mark scheme.
Both forward and electrically driven reverse processes are stated, five choices are visible, and the two-select control is correctly keyed to Combustion and Reversible.
The reversible ionic equation and all equilibrium statements are visible; equal forward and reverse rates is present and correctly keyed.
Inspected Figure 3 contains the complete potable-water and sewage-treatment flow, labels and internal separation diagram; the level descriptors, checklist and model cover both processes.
Inspected Figure 4 visibly distinguishes the two dye travel distances in water, and the attraction-to-paper explanation matches the displayed result.
Figure 4 clearly labels water and ethanol and shows the different yellow-dye positions; the solubility/attraction comparison is appropriately graded.
Inspected Figures 4 and 5 visibly show two spots for A and one for B, fully supporting the mixture-versus-pure comparison and its key.
The Rf value, dye distance and cm answer unit are supplied, with working space; the standard Rf rearrangement and 11.25 cm answer are correct.
The prompt is self-contained in the carried chromatography context, and the constant distance ratio/direct proportionality evidence correctly grades the explanation.
Two distinct labelled response fields are available, and the official sensitivity, accuracy/resolution and speed alternatives support any two valid advantages.
The fuel-burning setting and open response are clear; the high-temperature reaction of atmospheric nitrogen and oxygen is correctly represented by the model and checklist.
The rendered Table 2 includes complete fuel, energy and emission values with units; zero carbon dioxide for hydrogen supports the keyed positive climate impact.
Table 2 visibly shows the higher nitrogen-oxide mass for hydrogen, and the acid-rain/respiratory consequence evidence grades the requested negative impact.
The balanced equation, 20% oxygen figure, hydrogen volume, working space and dm³ unit are all visible; the 8.75 dm³ calculation is correct.
The crude-oil vapour and fractionating-column context resolves the referent, and the temperature-gradient, boiling-range and condensation evidence grades the explanation.

Table 3 visibly provides raw material, cost, strength, mass, lifespan and disposal data for both frames; the level-based evidence supports a reasoned evaluation rather than requiring every indicative point.
The aluminium-alloy bicycle-frame referent is visible through the prior subpart, and the protective oxide barrier explanation fits the two-mark task.
The iron-alloy chain and exclusion of paint are stated; the open response and accepted grease, oil, galvanising or stainless-steel solutions fit.
The carbon-fibre/polymer-resin composite is identified and separate labelled fields render; reinforcement and matrix/binder are the correct paired descriptions.
The sulfate-ion test prompt and separate test/result fields are visible, and barium chloride or nitrate with a white precipitate is correctly supported.
The inspected yield graph has complete axes, scale and curve, all four options render, and the smaller-yield-at-higher-temperature/exothermic statement is correctly keyed.
The complete reversible gaseous equation is visible and supports the three-moles-to-two-moles comparison and rightward equilibrium shift in the key.
The operating conditions and already-high 86% yield are visible with two response fields; the official cost, energy, safety and marginal-yield reasons support two distinct answers.
The inspected periodic table is complete and readable, all four metal options render, and Vanadium is present and correctly keyed as the likely transition-metal catalyst.
Inspected Figure 8 is a complete displayed-formula response canvas, and the C=C target description correctly identifies the alkene group to circle.
The visible context states that compound A contains an alkene and provides open response space; the accepted initial colours and decolourisation result fit the observation task.
The context explains that Figure 9 repeats Figure 8, and inspected Figure 9 is present as the actual annotatable canvas; the complete –O–C(=O)– target fits the ester task.

The molecular formula and four editable equation positions are visible; the keyed formulae and coefficient ratio 2:9:8:6 correctly balance complete combustion, while the mark evidence preserves partial credit.

The inspected official monomer-to-polymer skeleton contains both incomplete target positions, and the two response fields explicitly collect the missing single backbone bond and terminal n; the key matches both.
The carried displayed-formula equation visibly shows alkene monomers joining without a small-molecule product, all choices render, and Addition polymer is correctly keyed.
The melts-when-heated property and open line are explicit, and both thermosoftening and thermoplastic are supported by the grading evidence and model.
The prior polymer context resolves the comparison, response lines render, and covalent cross-links plus the high energy needed to overcome them correctly cover both marks.
The full method and inspected apparatus make the stopper timing visible; the key correctly explains that reaction starts before sealing and hydrogen escapes.
Inspected Figure 11 has complete readable inverted scales and time labels, and the supplied molar gas volume plus working field supports the correctly keyed 14 cm³ and 5.8 × 10⁻⁴ mol calculation.

Inspected Figure 12 is a complete graph canvas, and the response metadata supplies the tangent canvas, seven working lines, a labelled standard-form final-answer field and mol/s. The official checklist validly grades a result relative to the learner's tangent rather than imposing one fixed number.

Inspected Figure 13 provides the complete 0.40 mol/dm³ comparison curve on an annotatable graph; the lower initial gradient and 0.0084 mol plateau criteria fit the stated halved acid concentration and excess zinc.
The zinc–sulfuric acid reaction is visible through prior context, response lines render, and the rate, particle-energy and collision explanation correctly covers all three marks.

rationale: All 47 assembled contexts are learner-solvable. Structured tables provide the required values and units, every referenced local source or response image is present and readable, fixed controls contain viable correct responses, and written answers have fitting checklist/model evidence. The tangent-dependent hybrid response in 09.3 is supported by its canvas, working/final-answer metadata and official relative-gradient marking criteria.
